meaning: adj.可保证的,可证明的,可确认的
examples: Go to the library on weekends? You are a certifiable bookworm.
Non-certifiable: Agencies generally do not approve, recommend, or validate particular solutions.
His certifiable influence accounts for his importance, although whether he won the war on the obvious is yet to be determined.
The same concept applies to online education: consumers are willing to pay much more for media that provides certifiable education than they pay for a Netflix movie.
